task_completion.description: Verify the USLCI database has been imported into openLCA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The openLCA Navigation panel shows the USLCI database loaded in the 'Databases' tree.

task_completion.description: Verify a steel manufacturing process was cloned and renamed for the specific facility.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A process tab or navigation tree entry in openLCA shows a steel process with ' - Facility XYZ' appended to its original name (e.g., 'Steel, plate, at plant - Facility XYZ').

task_completion.description: Verify the Electricity input for the new process was reduced.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Inputs/Outputs' tab of the cloned process in openLCA shows the 'Electricity' exchange amount has been modified (should be 20% lower than the original).

task_completion.description: Verify the Natural gas (or primary fuel) input for the new process was reduced.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Inputs/Outputs' tab of the cloned process in openLCA shows the 'Natural gas' or fuel exchange amount has been modified (should be 12% lower than the original).

task_completion.description: Verify the process customization report CSV was created in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the file 'process_customization_report.csv' located in the '~/LCA_Results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the exported report contains the required original and new data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'process_customization_report.csv' (viewed in a text editor, terminal, or spreadsheet app) display the original process name, the new process name ('... - Facility XYZ'), and the original and new numerical amounts for both Electricity and Natural gas/fuel.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the openLCA graphical interface to clone and modify the process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the openLCA application open with the process editor active, rather than just terminal commands or Python scripts modifying database files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the report contains actual numerical data from the database, not placeholder text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV report should contain specific, realistic numerical values for the electricity and gas amounts, indicating they were genuinely read and calculated from the openLCA process.

integrity.description: Verify the agent cloned the process rather than just renaming the original.
integrity.visual_evidence: The openLCA navigation tree or the final report should indicate that both the original steel process and the new ' - Facility XYZ' process exist as distinct entities.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The USLCI database must be imported from '/home/ga/LCA_Imports/uslci_database.zip'. The agent must select a steel manufacturing process (which contains electricity and natural gas inputs) and clone it, appending ' - Facility XYZ' to the name. The new process must have its Electricity input reduced by exactly 20% (target ratio 0.8) and its Natural gas/fuel input reduced by exactly 12% (target ratio 0.88). The final report must be saved to '/home/ga/LCA_Results/process_customization_report.csv' and explicitly list the original and new process names, alongside the original and new amounts for both energy inputs.
